PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS TO DEFENDANT DOROTHY CALVIN'S JUNE 26, 2008 DECLARATION TESTIMONY IN OPPOSITION TO PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF CLASSIS OF CENTRAL CALIFORNIA

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANTS MIRALOMA COMMUNITY CHURCH, CONSISTORY OF MIRALOMA COMMUNITY CHURCH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AND DENIES THE DEFENDANTS' WITH RESPECT TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. COURT IS PREPARED TO ISSUE AN INJUNCTION CONSISTENT WITH THE RULING. OTHERWISE DENY THE PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS IT HAS NOT CARRIED ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT ITS RIGHTS ARE CONTRACTUAL OR BASED ON PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL. THE COURT DENIES THE CHURCHES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS THERE ARE OPEN QUESTIONS AS TO THE EXISTENCE OF ANY TRUST AND WHATEVER OBLIGATIONS MAY DERIVE FROM THAT. THE COURT GRANTS THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE CONVERSION CLAIM. THE COURT DOES NOT THINK PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT WITH RESPECT TO THE EXISTENCE OF CLAIMS UNDER THOSE PROVISIONS. THE COURT OTHERWISE DENIES THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR # 8189

LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF CLASSIS OF CENTRAL CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO 1ST, 2ND, 6TH, 7TH, 8TH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AND DENIES THE DEFENDANTS' WITH RESPECT TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. COURT IS PREPARED TO ISSUE AN INJUNCTION CONSISTENT WITH THE RULING. OTHERWISE DENY THE PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS IT HAS NOT CARRIED ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT ITS RIGHTS ARE CONTRACTUAL OR BASED ON PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL. THE COURT DENIES THE CHURCHES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS THERE ARE OPEN QUESTIONS AS TO THE EXISTENCE OF ANY TRUST AND WHATEVER OBLIGATIONS MAY DERIVE FROM THAT. THE COURT GRANTS THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE CONVERSION CLAIM. THE COURT DOES NOT THINK PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT WITH RESPECT TO THE EXISTENCE OF CLAIMS UNDER THOSE PROVISIONS. THE COURT OTHERWISE DENIES THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR # 8189

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-16-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
ALAN E. FRIEDMAN, ESQ. AND GEOFFREY P. FORGIONE (JONES DAY), (213) 489-3939, FOR PLAINTIFF AND CROSS-DEFENDANT CLASSIS OF CENTRAL CALIFORNIA
PETER SEAN BRADLEY, ESQ. AND RANDALL M. PENNER, ESQ. (PENNER, BRADLEY AND BUETTNER), (559) 221-2100, FOR DEFENDANTS/CROSS-COMPLAINANTS MIRALOMA COMMUNITY CHURCH, CONSISTORY OF MIRALOMA COMMUNITY CHURCH, DOROTHY CALVIN, MICHAEL KASKEY, GREGORY KAZARIAN AND PAT RYAN

THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. DEFENDANTS DOROTHY CALVIN, MICHAEL KASKEY, GREGORY KAZARIAN, PAT RYAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AND DENIES THE DEFENDANTS' WITH RESPECT TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. COURT IS PREPARED TO ISSUE AN INJUNCTION CONSISTENT WITH THE RULING. OTHERWISE DENY THE PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS IT HAS NOT CARRIED ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT ITS RIGHTS ARE CONTRACTUAL OR BASED ON PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL. THE COURT DENIES THE CHURCHES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS THERE ARE OPEN QUESTIONS AS TO THE EXISTENCE OF ANY TRUST AND WHATEVER OBLIGATIONS MAY DERIVE FROM THAT. THE COURT GRANTS THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE CONVERSION CLAIM. THE COURT DOES NOT THINK PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT WITH RESPECT TO THE EXISTENCE OF CLAIMS UNDER THOSE PROVISIONS. THE COURT OTHERWISE DENIES THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR # 8189 (301/PJB/LB)

Order To Show Cause Re Contempt
Off Calendar Oct-16-2007 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff Classis of Central California's ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE REGARDING CONTEMPT IS DENIED. HIRING AND PAYING A PASTOR IS WITHIN THE ORDINARY COURSE OF CHURCH BUSINESS. WHETHER THE HIRINGS COMPLY WITH THE BYLAWS IS OUTSIDE THE SCOPE OF THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =301/PJB/PM

Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction And Temporary Restraining Order
Off Calendar Apr-12-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Alan E. Friedman and Geoffrey P. Forgione of Jones Day, 213-489-3939, appearing for plaintiff and moving party
Peter Sean Bradley of the Law Offices of Penner Bradley & Buettner, 559-221-2100, appearing for defendant and responding party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff has demonstrated a likelihood of success on the merits and risk of irreparable injury if no interim relief is awarded. The proposed Preliminary Injunction is granted, modified to make it clear that defendant remains free to spend the funds it controls to defend this litigation. Parties are to meet and confer in preparing a proposed form of order. The temporary restraining order will remain in effect. (301/PJB/PM) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189
